frontpage.headline: World leaders are heading to New Delhi for India's AI Impact Summit next week
frontpage.summary: Top government leaders from multiple countries are confirmed to attend the India-AI Impact Summit in New Delhi, underlining India's push to position itself as a key player in global AI governance. The summit follows India's G20 AI Principles work and arrives as major powers compete to set the rules for the technology.
frontpage.takeaway: India hosting a heads-of-state AI summit means Delhi wants to write AI rules, not merely host AI markets.
frontpage.why: Directly relevant: the summit will shape how AI governance frameworks land in India. If you are building toward strategy or AI-generalist roles, understanding the regulatory and diplomatic context is table stakes. Watch for announcements on data governance, compute access, and India's own AI regulation posture — those will set the constraints or create the tailwinds for the next decade of Indian AI deployment.

stories.headline: Odisha signs a 1 GW renewable energy joint venture with NLC India
stories.summary: The Odisha government has signed a joint venture agreement with NLC India Renewables to develop 1 gigawatt of renewable energy capacity. The deal accelerates Odisha's clean energy transition, which has historically been dominated by coal power feeding large industrial consumers.
stories.takeaway: A major coal state signing a 1 GW renewable JV signals India's energy transition is reaching even its most fossil-dependent economies.
stories.why: Odisha has significant industrial power demand from steel and aluminium plants. If it can shift even a portion of that load to renewables, it moves the needle on India's overall grid emissions profile. For readers tracking India's energy transition, state-level JVs like this are where the actual gigawatts get built — the national targets live or die in state execution.

opportunities.name: PW AI Startup Challenge 2026
opportunities.when: Applications open now — deadline not specified, check link
opportunities.summary: An India-national AI startup competition run by Physics Wallah. Aimed at AI founders and builders. Worth applying if you are working on an AI product — national in scope, open to applicants across India.
